useful_note: The build spec is detailed (input shape, output shape with register notes, regional flavor, literal back-translation for fit-checking, fallback path) — that's substantively excellent. But the reply is one terse sentence with zero QR chips and no acknowledgment that this is a different shape from t1 (no near-match in gallery). The user committed quickly but the reply skipped over the 'no template exists for this — building from scratch' framing the surface map prescribed.
right_data_source_note: Correctly skipped template_suggestions for a no-match intent and went to build_oneshot. The 'narrow, self-contained, no connectors needed' framing is the right shape-recognition.
no_hallucinated_note: No invented templates, no claimed capabilities outside scope.
op_correctness_note: build_oneshot fired (one of the allowed pivots from a no-match intent). No template_suggestions garbage.
overall_note: Right op, terse delivery. Could have done a one-line acknowledgment 'no near-match in the gallery for this — building from scratch' to make the contrast with t1 explicit, but the spec quality compensates.
